summary: @vite-tab/tab impersonates vite and executes an obfuscated payload from bin/vite.js
details: Package `@vite-tab/tab@5.7.0` impersonates the legitimate `vite` build tool: `package.json` copies vite's name shape, sets `author` to 'Evan You', reuses vite's description and homepage (https://vitejs.dev), and declares `bin.vite` pointing at `bin/vite.js`. The CLI entrypoint `bin/vite.js` appends an obfuscated block after the legitimate-looking CLI code. The block implements a bespoke string-shuffle decoder (custom-alphabet Fisher-Yates keyed by numeric seeds 3603173 and 778762 with `%`, `#1`, `#0` substitutions) that decodes identifier names (`require`, `module`, `__dirname`, `__filename`), attaches them to `global`, then resolves `Function` via a decoded property lookup and invokes `Function(...)` on a large decoded string body. This runs every time the `vite` bin is executed. The package also declares runtime dependencies unusual for a build tool — `@solana/web3.js`, `axios`, `socket.io-client`, `form-data` — consistent with network exfiltration and crypto-wallet targeting. Installing this package and running its `vite` shim executes attacker-controlled code on the developer machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
